assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md: 'Classification: code change request' and 'Action: Sub-task created to address this feedback.' subtask-30001.md exists and addresses comment 30001. report.md: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping)' and 'Sub-task created for comment 30001.'

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: 'Classification: suggestion'. Reasoning explains suggestive language: 'The language uses "should" but frames the recommendation as a performance optimization ("Queries filtering by `deleted_at IS NULL` will be frequent and a partial index would help").' The file explicitly explains why it is a suggestion rather than a code change request, covering both the suggestive language and the performance-optimization nature of the feedback.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md: 'Classification: nit' and 'Action: No sub-task created.' No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md: 'Classification: question' and 'Action: No sub-task created.' No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, 'trustify-backend'), '## Description' (line 7), '## Files to Modify' (line 10), '## Implementation Notes' (line 13), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 25).

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md line 38: '## Target PR' followed by line 39: 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md lines 33-35: '## Review Context' section containing 'Reviewer reviewer-a (comment 30001)' and the quoted original review comment text: '> The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...'

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md), one sub-task description (subtask-30001.md), and the report (report.md). No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) were created or appear modified. All output files are markdown analysis/report files consistent with read-only verification behavior.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments are classified with individual output files (review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md) and all four are summarized in the report.md Review Feedback row: '1 code change request (comment 30001: transaction wrapping), 1 suggestion (comment 30002: index), 1 nit (comment 30003: context message), 1 question (comment 30004: GET behavior).'

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no merge commands, no auto-merge statements, and the overall verdict is 'WARN' which would not trigger auto-merge. The report ends with a standard footer and contains no indication that any merge action was taken.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in a new file (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs) with 5 new test functions. No existing tests modified or deleted. |'

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md explicitly evaluates convention upgrade eligibility: 'No convention evidence was found to upgrade this to a code change request. CONVENTIONS.md for the trustify-backend repository was not available for inspection, and the PR diff contains only this single migration, providing no basis for establishing a codebase-wide pattern of index creation on filter columns.' and 'Per the Style/Conventions sub-agent analysis, upgrading based on general industry best practices ("indexes are a database best practice") is not sufficient -- upgrade requires a concrete CONVENTIONS.md section or a counted codebase pattern.' report.md also confirms: 'Comment 30002 (suggestion -- not upgraded): Adding an index on `deleted_at` is a valid performance suggestion but was not upgraded to a code change request because no CONVENTIONS.md evidence or counted codebase pattern was available to justify the upgrade.'

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md: 'Classification: suggestion' and 'Action: No sub-task created.' No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. The reasoning explicitly states no convention evidence was found to upgrade: 'No convention evidence was found to upgrade this to a code change request.'

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (5 tests with distinct algorithms). Test Documentation: PASS (all test functions have doc comments). Eval Quality: N/A (no eval results). |' Eval Quality is explicitly listed as N/A with the explanation 'no eval results', and the Test Quality combination result is PASS without being affected by Eval Quality.
